theme_and_archetype.description: Industrial process control requires authority, clarity, and truth. The interface acts as a high-density, highly-readable control room. It employs an 'Industrial Precision' style: completely monochromatic UI where color is used strictly for functional signaling (alerts, status).

scale.kpi_value: text-4xl sm:text-5xl font-light tracking-tighter font-mono text-zinc-950
scale.labels_and_overlines: text-[10px] sm:text-xs uppercase tracking-[0.2em] font-bold text-zinc-500

rules: No generic sans-serif for numbers. Use IBM Plex Mono for all KPIs, tables, and sensor values.
Use radical weight contrast: extremely light weights for massive numbers/headings, bold weights for tiny labels.
Never center-align dense data or paragraphs. Strictly left-aligned or right-aligned for numbers.
95% of the UI must be monochrome (zinc/white).
Color is Function Only. Never use primary blue or semantic colors for decoration.
Signal red, warning yellow, and safety green are reserved strictly for machine status, RCA alerts, and condition monitoring.

layout_and_spacing.container: w-full max-w-[1920px] mx-auto p-4 md:p-6 lg:p-8
rules: F-Pattern hierarchy: Place the most critical plant metrics (OEE, Status) at the top left.
Zero dead space. Use `h-full` to ensure widgets stretch to match neighbors.
Containers with borders must have internal padding of `p-4` or `p-6`.
Expose the skeleton. No soft shadows, no glassmorphism.
Use `rounded-none` or absolute minimum `rounded-sm` (2px) for all elements including buttons and inputs.
Containers should use a sharp 1px border `border-zinc-200` with flat white background.
Hovering a clickable card should darken the border to `border-zinc-400` and apply `-translate-y-[1px]`, avoiding heavy shadows.
Animations must be minimal and crisp, simulating rapid machine response.
Transition duration should be fast: `duration-150 ease-out`.
Focus states are critical for accessibility in a dense UI: `focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-[#002FA7] focus:ring-offset-2`.

component_rules.buttons: Square edges (`rounded-none`). High contrast text. Use icon + text heavily for clarity.
component_rules.tables: Dense rows. Right-align all numeric data. Use subtle zebra striping (`even:bg-zinc-50/50`) to improve scannability of sensor logs.
component_rules.forms_and_inputs: Square borders. Label above input. Use Shadcn customized to `rounded-none`.
component_rules.rag_interface: Distinguish AI responses from user queries using background tints. User: `bg-white border border-zinc-200`. AI: `bg-zinc-50 border border-[#002FA7]/20 border-l-4 border-l-[#002FA7]`. Include structured block areas for Root Cause, Actions, and References.
component_rules.charts: Use Recharts. Minimal grid lines (dashed, faint). Monochromatic bars/lines unless representing status thresholds (where red/yellow lines indicate limits).
component_rules.data_testid: MANDATORY on all interactive and key informational elements. Example: `data-testid="rag-submit-button"` or `data-testid="kpi-oee-value"`.

iconography.style: Use `weight="regular"` for standard icons, `weight="fill"` for active states or critical status indicators. Keep sizes uniform (e.g., 16px or 20px) to align with dense text.

hero_background.description: Subtle blueprint architectural texture. Apply at extremely low opacity (e.g., 3-5%) mixed with bg-zinc-50 for a technical watermark background in empty spaces or login screens.

control_room_scene.description: Engineer monitoring screens in a manufacturing plant. Use for auth/login page hero or as a placeholder for a 'Plant Overview' report cover.

machinery_overview.description: Modern factory interior showcasing industrial machinery. Useful for 'Plant Area' visual navigation or header banners in specific modules.

instructions_to_main_agent: Load Chivo, IBM Plex Sans, and IBM Plex Mono via Google Fonts in index.html or global css.
Install and use @phosphor-icons/react for all icons.
Strictly adhere to the `rounded-none` and sharp grid border styling to maintain the Archetype 4 Swiss industrial look.
For the RAG Interface, build a structured view that breaks down responses into 'Summary', 'Root Cause', 'Recommended Action', and 'References'. Do not just dump text.
Implement condition monitoring data with Recharts, keeping axes minimal and emphasizing the actual data line/bar.
Use grid layouts with zero nested gaps where possible (employ border-collapse strategies to expose the skeleton).
Ensure the application works seamlessly on wide screens (Control Room monitors) by using `max-w-[1920px]` and stretching columns.
Add `data-testid` attributes to all forms, buttons, links, and major display widgets.
